task: Compare available plans for the AeroAPI on Flightaware.
graderReasoning: The agent completed the task by navigating to FlightAware’s AeroAPI page and extracting a comparison of the available plans. The trajectory shows the correct page URL (https://www.flightaware.com/commercial/aeroapi/) with the plan comparison visible on screen: Personal, Standard, and Premium, along with their included features, monthly minimums, rate limits, support levels, and usage permissions. The final response accurately summarizes these plan differences and pricing structure, and the cited details are supported by the page snapshot and fetched page content.
final_result_response:
I found three AeroAPI plans on FlightAware: Personal, Standard, and Premium. FlightAware says tiers should be chosen based on “use case and data needs,” including usage and storage limits. <citation refs="fetch_call_kodfZX0oGRcE9xfHGRfRzGab|fc_08cc73367c2b8f47016a044372c8848199858a734187711953#1">“Choose the appropriate tier based on your use case and data needs, taking note of limitations on usage and storage restrictions.”</citation>

| Plan | Best for | Includes | Pricing / limits |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Personal** | Personal or academic use only | Current flight status and tracks, static maps/imagery | Per-query fees, up to **$5 free/month** or **$10 free/month for ADS-B feeders**; **no monthly minimum**; **10 result sets/minute** |
| **Standard** | Business internal use or B2C use | Everything in Personal, plus **historical data**, **flight alerting**, **email support** | Per-query fees with **$100/month minimum**; **5 result sets/second**; historical limit **500,000 result sets/month**; volume discounts |
| **Premium** | B2B commercial use, higher-volume or enterprise needs | Everything in Standard, plus **Aireon space-based ADS-B**, **FlightAware Foresight predictions**, phone support, invoicing, 99.5% uptime guarantee | Per-query fees with **$1,000/month minimum**; **100 result sets/second**; historical limit **500,000 result sets/month**; volume discounts |

Key differences: Standard is the first plan with historical data and alerts; Premium is required for Foresight, Aireon space-based ADS-B, B2B commercialization, phone support, invoicing, and uptime guarantee. <citation refs="fetch_call_kodfZX0oGRcE9xfHGRfRzGab|fc_08cc73367c2b8f47016a044372c8848199858a734187711953#1">“FlightAware Foresight… Included in Premium”; “Aireon Space-Based ADS-B… Included in Premium”; “Historical Flight Data… Included in Standard… Included in Premium”; “Commercialization for Business to Business (B2B)… Included in Premium.”</citation>

Pricing is usage-based per result set, and FlightAware notes that one result set equals 15 records. Standard and Premium are eligible for volume discounts, while Personal is not. <citation refs="fetch_call_kodfZX0oGRcE9xfHGRfRzGab|fc_08cc73367c2b8f47016a044372c8848199858a734187711953#1">“Pricing is based on result sets, with one set equaling 15 records”; “All Premium and Standard tier accounts are eligible for volume discounting.”</citation>
action_history: Opening FlightAware: const p1 = await openTab('https://www.flightaware.com/');
